subject: [PATCH v2 1/7] selftests/cgroup: skip test_zswap if zswap is globally disabled

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested consolidating the logic for checking zswap configuration into a single helper function, check_zswap_enabled(), which would read and parse the enabled value in one place.

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the logic for skipping a test when zswap is globally disabled by consolidating the skip and fail conditions into one place.

reviews.author: Li Wang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Li Wang suggested consolidating the logic for deciding whether to skip or fail the test based on zswap_enabled() in one place, and proposed opening up the code if no other callers exist.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, pass it as &value works well.

Patchset v3 is on the way :).

-- 
Regards,
Li Wang

reviews.author: Li Wang
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that ksft_exit_skip() is used inconsistently in zswap_enabled(), sometimes returning a value and other times using it to decide whether to skip or fail, and suggested consolidating the logic into one place.
reviews.raw_body:
And use sizeof(&value) just get the pointer's size but not
the buffer size, so I would recommend: char value[2].

-- 
Regards,
Li Wang

2026-03-13.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the logic for skipping a test when zswap is globally disabled by consolidating the skip and fail conditions into one place.
